question.text: Under the Indian Trust Act 1882, a trust is defined as an obligation annexed to the ownership of property. Section 6 specifies the essential elements of a valid trust. Which of the following is NOT one of the 5 essential elements?

answer.text: Registration of the trust deed with the Charity Commissioner

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Sec 6, the 5 essential elements of a valid trust are: (1) Intention to create trust, (2) Lawful purpose, (3) Ascertainable beneficiary, (4) Trust property, and (5) Transfer of property to trustee. Registration with the Charity Commissioner is NOT an essential element under Sec 6. **The Trap:** Candidates familiar with public trusts may assume registration is essential. While public trusts may require registration under state-specific laws, it is not one of the 5 Sec 6 essentials. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM X-B tests the Indian Trust Act 1882 provisions. The three parties are: Author/Settlor (creator), Trustee (manager), and Beneficiary (recipient). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When clients consider setting up a trust, explain all 5 essential elements. Many trusts fail because the "intention" or "beneficiary" is not clearly documented.
